steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The blank sits between the noun 'artists' and the names 'Thomas Hart Benton and Jackson Pollock.' The names directly identify which artists are meant — they form a single, tightly bound noun phrase: US artists Thomas Hart Benton and Jackson Pollock.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
When a specifying noun phrase (the names) is essential to identifying the noun (artists), no punctuation separates them — the same way we write my friend Sarah, not my friend, Sarah,.

Choice A inserts a colon, but a colon needs a complete independent clause before it. Choice C adds a stray comma between the two names. Choice D adds a comma that wrongly treats the names as a non-essential aside.

The answer is B.
